articles.title: VoidZero is Joining Cloudflare
articles.description: One of the year’s big moves in JS tooling, as told by Evan himself. The company he founded to build Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, etc. is joining Cloudflare. He’s candid about why (monetization is hard). Good news: all projects stay MIT-licensed, and Evan and co remain in charge.
articles.author: Evan You

articles.title: Announcing Angular v22
articles.description: Google’s batteries-included framework graduates three big features to stable/production-ready: Signal Forms, Angular Aria, and asynchronous reactivity. The router also adds experimental support for the native browser Navigation API.
articles.author: The Angular Team

articles.title: Modern Engineering Values
articles.description: “I cannot believe that I used to write code by hand!” The creator of Jest and Fate says that as agents write more software, the engineering values that matter shift, with ownership, taste, and strict-but-fast feedback loops at the fore.
articles.author: Christoph Nakazawa

articles.title: 'I Wish Deno Would Keep Doing What It Does Best'
articles.description: A Deno fan argues that chasing Node.js/npm compatibility is costing the runtime its edge as a zero-config, web-standards, all-in-one-binary experience.
articles.author: Hong Minhee

articles.title: What Bun Can Tell Us About AI and Open Source
articles.description: An analyst looks at Bun, whose code is increasingly AI-produced rather than human-written, as a bellwether for whether AI-authored core infrastructure is sustainable.
articles.author: Stephen O'Grady (RedMonk)

articles.title: Geometric.js: A Library for Doing Geometry
articles.description: Created by someone who works on graphics for the NYT, this elegant library lets you work with polygons, bounding boxes, reflection, interpolation, rotation, and the like (examples). Does one polygon intersect with another? There’s a function for that.
articles.author: Harry Stevens (The New York Times)

articles.title: Micromodal.js: A Tiny Library for Accessible Modal Dialogs
articles.description: No-dependency library for WAI-ARIA guideline-compliant modal dialogs. Offers extra polish versus <dialog>, with hooks, scroll-locking, in/out transitions, and click backdrop to dismiss.
articles.author: Ghosh and Singh

articles.title: 📝 PDFSlick 4.0: View and Interact with PDFs in JS Apps
articles.description: A PDF viewer for React, Solid, Svelte & vanilla JS apps that goes from simple PDF viewing up to working with multiple, large documents with annotations. Demo.
articles.author: Vancho Stojkov

articles.title: babel-plugin-zod-hoist: Hoist Zod Schema Definitions to the Top of the File
articles.description: From the creator of Slonik comes a way to make Zod validation significantly faster.
articles.author: Gajus Kuizinas

articles.description: Safari 27 (beta), announced at WWDC, includes a standards-compliant ESM loader rewrite improving top-level await support, and adds support for JSPI (JavaScript Promise Integration) in WebAssembly.
Electron 43 is in beta with big perf boosts, thanks to an embedded Node.js snapshot, bundles cached as V8 bytecode, and link-time optimization on the main binary.
The Rust port of React Compiler is ready to test (Meta is already seeing "great results") – it's set to be merged imminently.
TanStack Table v9 Beta – A powerful headless engine for creating data grids with support for React, Preact, Solid, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and Lit.
Node-RED 5.0 – 'Low-code' flow programming environment, built on Node.js.
Rolldown 1.1, pnpm 11.5, RunJS 4, Emscripten 6.0

articles.title: What's New in WebKit in Safari 27 Beta
articles.description: Apple’s WWDC is taking place this week, and Safari 27, coming to iPhone, iPad, and Mac users later this year, is now in beta. It's a big release with 58 new features, from customizable <select> elements to numerous new CSS properties and sizes="auto" support for <img> elements. ▶️ Jen Simmons presents a video roundup.
articles.author: WebKit

articles.title: How Building an HTML-First Site Doubled Our Users Overnight
articles.description: After two costly failed rebuilds, a public-service application flow was rebuilt around HTML forms, backend-saved progress, and JavaScript used only where it helped, which doubled form completions overnight.
articles.author: Alistair Davidson

articles.title: Context-Aware Headings in HTML
articles.description: Manuel explores a new experimental HTML feature called the headingoffset attribute, which allows us to dynamically adjust heading levels based on their nesting depth. “The implementation, as it stands, seems very sensible to me”.
articles.author: Manuel Matuzović

articles.title: The Fundamentals and Dev Experience of CSS @function
articles.description: @function lets you define functions across stylesheets that return a value usable wherever CSS can use one. Jane finds today’s dev experience rough (and support is limited to Chromium-based browsers for now) but sees the potential.
articles.author: Jane Ori

articles.title: CSS is Filling the Gaps with Rules: A Way to Style Gaps in Grid and Flex
articles.description: A detailed look at the extent of what gap decorations can do with code & demos, plus practical ideas of how to use the feature in practice.
articles.author: Brecht De Ruyte

articles.title: CSS Can Now Animate Between Pages: View Transitions Without JavaScript
articles.description: How cross-document view transitions can bring app-like page transitions to any website with just one CSS rule, no JS needed.
articles.author: David Krcek

articles.title: You Probably Shouldn’t Be Annotating Focus Order
articles.description: If your HTML is semantic and follows reading order, focus order takes care of itself.
articles.author: Eric Bailey

articles.title: Scrubbable Staggered Animation with CSS @function
articles.description: A step-by-step look at using a mathematical formula to create a reusable, predictable staggered effect, be that for an animation, scroll progress, or any other input.
articles.author: Ashutosh Biswas

articles.title: ▶  Rediscover the HTML Select Element
articles.description: The select element has had a glow-up of late, and this video shows us it in action. You can now take this default element and have it visually match your site's styling.
articles.author: Tim Nguyen (Apple Developer)

articles.title: ▶  Create Web Extensions for Safari
articles.description: A half-hour deep dive into building and testing a web extension for Safari, without the need to touch Xcode.
articles.author: Kiara Rose (Apple Developer)

articles.title: ▶  Learn CSS Grid Lanes
articles.description: A ten-minute introduction looking at how grid lanes can be used to arrange differently-shaped elements into clean, flexible layouts.
articles.author: Brandon Stewart (Apple Developer)

articles.title: ▶  Web Dev Challenge: Rebuild Your Website with ATproto
articles.description: Contestants get time to plan and integrate ATproto into their sites. I’ve been tinkering around with this. A fun way to learn about it!
articles.author: CodeTV

articles.title: The Field Guide to CSS Grid Lanes
articles.description: The WebKit team has just launched this detailed guide to Grid Lanes. It’s a clearly structured set of interactive playgrounds and demos highlighting how this relatively new CSS feature can be used. A good reference worth bookmarking.
articles.author: WebKit

articles.title: NoLoJS: HTML/CSS Alternatives to Common JS Patterns
articles.description: A growing collection of common JS patterns that can now be replaced with HTML, CSS, or very little JS. Think accordions, modals, lazy loading, etc.
articles.author: Aaron T. Grogg

articles.title: 🔠 FontSelf: A Tool to Configure Google Fonts for Self-Hosting
articles.description: You might be self-hosting fonts already, but this could help speed up the process. You can select and configure your chosen font, download a package, and get the CSS.
articles.author: FontSelf

articles.title: .MD This Page: A Browser Extension to Convert Any Web Page to Clean Markdown
articles.description: Particularly useful for making content LLM-ready, removing ads, UI elements, etc. while maintaining a simple, clear hierarchy.
articles.author: Adem Kouki

articles.title: tsParticles 4: A Particle Engine for Web Effects
articles.description: If you want a confetti cannon, fireworks (July 4 is coming up soon), ribbons, fireflies, snow, or similar effects on your pages, this is for you. You can see some live demos here.
articles.author: Matteo Bruni
